purposes.description: Validate user input (email, URL, IP, phone, dates, passwords, UUIDs, semver) using correct regular expression patterns.

primitives.evidence: Validation patterns use groups, alternation, anchors, and common patterns for email, URL, date. The password pattern uses lookahead (L3-adjacent) but is provided as a template, not generated.

purposes.description: Extract structured data from log lines, code files, and unstructured text using regex patterns across multiple languages (Python, JavaScript, Go, grep/sed).

primitives.evidence: Log parsing patterns use lookahead/lookbehind (e.g., grep -oP '"level"\s*:\s*"\K[^"]+'), named groups, and complex multi-part patterns for Apache log format.
Python examples use re module (stdlib): re.match, re.search, re.findall, re.sub, re.compile, re.VERBOSE, re.MULTILINE, re.DOTALL flags.
JavaScript examples use built-in regex literals, String.match, String.matchAll, String.replace, String.split with regex.
Shell examples use grep -P/-E/-oP with pipes, sed with extended regex and capture groups, perl one-liners.

purposes.description: Perform code refactoring and text cleanup operations using regex-based search-and-replace across files.

primitives.evidence: Refactoring patterns use word boundaries (\b), capture group backreferences, and character class replacements.
Complex pipelines: grep -rlP | xargs sed, perl -pe with /ge modifier for snake_case to camelCase conversion, sed with multiple substitution commands.

purposes.description: Diagnose and fix regex patterns that do not match as expected, understanding greedy vs lazy, escaping, newline handling, and backtracking issues.

primitives.evidence: Gotchas section covers greedy vs lazy, escaping special characters, multiline/dotall flags, and catastrophic backtracking avoidance.
Debugging requires analyzing why a pattern fails: identifying greedy over-matching, missing escapes, or wrong flag usage.

purposes.description: Use the bundled regex_tester.py script to validate, test, explain, and generate regex patterns from the command line.

primitives.evidence: Running the script with various flags (--pattern, --text, --explain, --generate, --json) and parsing its output.
The script uses re, json, pathlib, argparse from stdlib. Agent may need to invoke or extend it.
